authority.registry_source: repo-governed tool_catalog.json and linked operator docs

environment_contract.notes: Operator tools should resolve DCOIR local configuration from Machine/System environment scope. Process-scope placeholders such as C:\path\to\dcoir-collector are invalid and should be rejected. Shared environment and path helpers live in modules/Dcoir.Common and git-lane self-contained helpers live in modules/Dcoir.Git.

module_ecosystem.role: paths, logging, JSON, validation, filesystem, safe names, timestamps, and shared context

self-contained git-lane helpers: Machine/System env lookup, placeholder rejection, UTF-8 logging, git executable discovery, native argument quoting, logged git process execution, branch checks, clean-tree checks, fetch, fast-forward pull, and ahead/behind analysis
module_ecosystem.validated_status: validated for git diagnostic, safe pre-pull, snapshot, and repo patch apply tools

module_ecosystem.role: gh auth, GitHub Actions API wrappers, run/job lookup
ChatGPT-friendly ZIP packaging wrapper and staging extension point
manifest parsing, dispatch, monitor, fail-fast gates, parallel execution throttle, capture, cleanup, summaries, exit codes
module_ecosystem.validated_status: validated as preferred Actions orchestrator lane

module_ecosystem.role: repo-relative path safety, safe names, path normalization, under-root checks, text-file filtering, binary sniffing, targeted staging, and UTF-8 logging
module_ecosystem.validated_status: validated by text-only and targeted snapshot smoke tests

module_ecosystem.role: repo patch path safety, payload-root resolution, allowed target roots, hashing, and UTF-8 logging
module_ecosystem.validated_status: validated by WhatIfOnly and real fixture apply smoke tests

module_ecosystem.role: Reusable operator-tool logging default: one uploadable log path, UTF-8 logging, phase transitions, error detail, safe configuration presence/source, and secret-safe output discipline.
module_ecosystem.validated_status: CAP/readback verified for operator logging default; referenced by tool_catalog_operator_logging_policy.json

tools.tool_name: DCOIR ChatGPT Apply-In Single Payload Builder
tools.notes: Preferred builder and validator for chatgpt-apply-in staging. Builds one ZIP with root apply_manifest.json and files/, base64 encodes it as exactly payload.zip.b64, validates ZIP open, manifest, files/, safe request id, no truncation marker, no parts or chunks, and base64 round trip. If connector limits are hit, reduce the batch size and rebuild a smaller single payload.zip.b64.
Validated. Imports Dcoir.Git for Machine/System env lookup, UTF-8 logging, native git invocation, branch/status, diff, stash, and graph diagnostics.

Validated. Imports Dcoir.Git for shared environment, UTF-8 logging, stash, fetch, fast-forward pull, safe new-stash detection, stash reapply, and conflict-safe stop behavior. Does not apply older existing stashes when no new stash is created.
Validated. Uses Dcoir.Git for git freshness and clean-tree checks and Dcoir.Snapshot for repo-relative path safety, targeted copy, and UTF-8 logging. Missing requested paths are hard failures.

Validated. Uses Dcoir.Git and Dcoir.Snapshot for read-only full-repo text snapshots, repo-relative paths, excluded directories/extensions, binary sniffing, generated manifest, and ChatGPT-friendly ZIP output.

Validated. Manifest-driven repo patch and apply helper using Dcoir.Git and Dcoir.RepoPatch. Supports payload root detection, wrapper-root stripping, explicit repo-relative copy maps, git rm deletion lists, allowed target roots, pre/post hash checks, PowerShell 5.1 compatibility, logs, and result JSON. WhatIfOnly and real fixture apply smokes passed.

Reusable ZIP helper for DCOIR diagnostics and upload bundles. Creates rootless ChatGPT-optimized ZIPs with forward-slash archive paths, priority triage entries first, diagnostic_index.md, captured_files.json, zip_manifest.json, hidden/system metadata filtering, optional UTF-8 normalization, and no wrapper-root junk.

Stable public entrypoint backed by reusable modules: Dcoir.Common, Dcoir.GitHub, Dcoir.Packaging, and Dcoir.Actions. Owns manifest parsing, dispatch guardrails, monitoring, fail-fast gates, parallel workflow execution throttle, evidence capture, artifact download, ChatGPT-friendly ZIP packaging, cleanup_output_folder_after_zip, summaries, and exit codes.
Harness-only wrapper. Imports Dcoir.Common for shared env, JSON, and logging helpers, creates dry-run, watch, and live guarded manifests, and executes the Actions Workflow Orchestrator.

Harness-only fail-fast same-workflow mode and suite ladder runner. Imports Dcoir.Common for shared env, JSON, and safe-name helpers, creates a sequential ladder manifest, and executes the orchestrator.

tools.output_pattern: ChatGPT apply-in payload staging folder and ZIP plus payload report under DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR
tools.notes: Active apply-in staging helper. Use for GitHub Actions apply-in payload preparation. Core skill parity runbook prefers bounded batches; if a four-skill batch fails, retry 2+2; one-skill payload only for natural final remainder or explicit operator-approved test or retry.

tools.output_pattern: caller-owned uploadable log path returned on success and failure
tools.notes: Module-level logging standard for operator tools. New or reworked local tools should prefer this module over ad hoc logging and should return an uploadable log path.

tools.output_pattern: dry-run or apply issue close logs and summaries
tools.notes: Guarded bulk issue closure helper. Dry-run first; use only with explicit operator intent and token and config preconditions satisfied.

tools.output_pattern: chatgpt exec request or report artifacts and output ZIPs
tools.version_or_hash: validated exec lane; example restage commit 5fdfe97b6f4296de2048a6330a9f8fb76d2b4a5e
tools.notes: Active ChatGPT exec lane harness. Validated sequence: create apply-in payload that writes one exec request JSON, verify apply-in report and exec request readback, then restage the already-created request JSON without [skip ci] if needed to trigger exec.

sweep_notes.purpose: Retired obsolete Airtable-only operator tooling and pruned the main catalog back to GitHub Desktop lane surfaces that still support the current Supabase, GitHub, and ChatGPT Agent construct.
known_limitations: Historical Airtable exports and status reports under chatgpt_staging are intentionally out of scope for this operator_tools retirement bundle.
A separate follow-on PR may remove now-unneeded staging inputs and outputs after workflow-path review confirms the required scaffolding to keep.
